$comment: SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH. scripts/build-handoff.mjs generates HANDOFF.md, handoff.html, and handover/*.json from this — never hand-edit the generated files. Direction documented: Centred classic (v=1). Copy is verbatim from the prototype.
title: Playground Sign-in
summary: The Playground educator sign-in journey: service login → educator select → PIN → room select → room hub, plus the returning-educator (same-day) path. This is the native rebuild's foundational auth flow. The documented direction is Centred classic (v=1): white surface, centred column, teal 'P' emblem, on real Stardust --sd-* tokens. 'Done' = a returning educator reaches their room hub in under four taps and every error state recovers without a dead end. Exact pixels live in the runnable prototype — each screen links to its state.
outOfScope: Room Hub screen itself (Phase 3 — separate spec); this flow ends by routing into it
Offline sync + token-refresh internals (handled by the offline architecture, Phase 1)

components.name: ds-input
components.notes: 52px tall, radius-lg, 1px grey-500 border; needs leading-icon (username) + trailing password-reveal toggle slots

components.name: ds-btn
components.notes: primary full-width 52px; loading state shows spinner + 'Signing in…'

components.name: ds-card
components.notes: educator rows + room rows; room rows carry a ratio badge and a selected (green) treatment

components.name: ds-message-box
components.notes: inline Alert banner (offline / closed-room) with optional action link

components.name: ds-pin-keypad
components.notes: NET-NEW: 4-digit PIN dots + on-screen numeric keypad with delete; auto-submits on the 4th digit. No system keyboard.

components.name: ds-state-panel
components.notes: NET-NEW: centred blocking/empty state — icon circle + title + body + optional secondary action. Used by app-disabled, no-access, no-rooms, lockout, bootstrap-failed.

motion.notes: launch splash ~1s on cold start; sign-in shows a ~1.15s loading state; prefers-reduced-motion freezes animated scenes

screens.name: Service sign-in
screens.purpose: Authenticate the device to a childcare service.
screens.exit: → S2 Educator select on success; inline error on failure (see e-creds)
layout: Centred column on white surface, max-width ~320
Service username field → Service password field

copy.text: Sign in to your service to get started.
By signing in you agree to our Terms of Service and Privacy Policy

redlines.spec: 52px tall, full-width, radius-lg, teal action-primary, white text

behaviour: Sign in is enabled only when both username and password are non-empty.
Tap Sign in → ~1.15s loading state (spinner + 'Signing in…') → success routes to S2.
Invalid credentials show the inline service error (e-creds) and keep the user on S1.
Password field has a trailing reveal toggle (show/hide).
Demo credentials in the prototype: username LittleBugs · password bugs123.
a11y: Both inputs have visible persistent labels; focus order top→bottom.
Password reveal toggle is a labelled button (aria-label 'Toggle password').
Inline error is announced (role=alert); error text contrast ≥4.5:1.

acceptance.text: Sign in is disabled until both fields have content.
Valid credentials show the loading state then route to S2.
Invalid credentials show the inline service error and stay on S1.
Password reveal toggle flips the field between masked and plain.

screens.name: Educator select
screens.purpose: Choose which educator is signing in to this service.
screens.exit: → S4 PIN on selecting an educator; → S3 Add educator on tapping add
behaviour: Selecting an educator carries it into context and routes to S4 PIN.
Search filters the list live; no match shows the empty line.
Sort toggles between most-recent-login and alphabetical.
Add-educator row opens S3.
a11y: Educator rows are buttons with accessible names (name + role); ≥44px targets.
Sort pills expose selected state; search field has a label.

acceptance.text: Selecting an educator routes to S4 carrying that educator.
A search with no matches shows the no-match line.

screens.name: Add educator profile
screens.purpose: Sign in a new educator profile not yet in the list.
screens.entry: From the add-educator row on S2
screens.exit: → S5 Room select on success; back returns to S2

copy.text: Please sign into your Educator Profile

redlines.spec: enabled only when both fields filled

behaviour: Sign in enabled only when both fields are non-empty.
Password/PIN field has a reveal toggle.
On success routes to S5 Room select.
a11y: Labelled inputs; reveal toggle is a labelled button; back nav is reachable.

acceptance.text: Sign in is disabled until both fields have content.

screens.name: PIN entry
screens.purpose: Verify the selected educator with a 4-digit PIN.
screens.entry: After selecting an educator on S2
screens.exit: → S5 Room select (cold start) / → Room Hub (returning) on correct PIN; lockout after repeated failures (e-locked)
behaviour: PIN auto-submits once the 4th digit is entered (no submit button).
Incorrect PIN shakes the dots and shows the incorrect-attempt message; attempts increment.
Repeated failures lead to a locked state (e-locked).
Demo PIN in the prototype: 1234.
Back returns to the educator list (or same-day for returning users).
a11y: Keypad keys are ≥44px labelled buttons; delete key labelled.
PIN progress is announced; the incorrect message is announced (role=alert).
Avoid relying on colour alone for the incorrect state (message + shake).

acceptance.text: Entering the 4th digit auto-submits without a button.
A correct PIN advances to S5 (cold start).
An incorrect PIN shows the incorrect message and increments attempts.

screens.name: Room select
screens.purpose: Choose the room the educator is working in today.
screens.entry: After a correct PIN (cold start) or Add educator
layout: List of room rows (name + ratio, selectable)

redlines.spec: selectable card with ratio badge; selected = green treatment
disabled until a room is selected

behaviour: Selecting a room enables Continue and updates its label to 'Continue to <Room>'.
Continue routes to the Room Hub.
A closed room shows a warning but can still be entered (see e-closed).
a11y: Room rows are radio-like selectable buttons with names + ratio; selected state is exposed non-visually.

acceptance.text: Continue is disabled until a room is selected.
Continue routes to the Room Hub with the selected room in context.

screens.name: Welcome back (same-day)
screens.purpose: Fast-path a returning educator straight to their remembered room.
screens.exit: → Room Hub on Continue; Change room → S5
behaviour: Shows the room remembered from the last session as pre-selected.
Continue routes to the Room Hub.
Change room opens the full S5 list.
a11y: Greeting heading receives focus on entry; Change room is a clearly labelled link.

acceptance.text: The remembered room is pre-selected and Continue routes to the hub.
Change room opens the full room list (S5).

stateMatrix.ui: inline error on the password field
stateMatrix.copy: We couldn't sign in to that service, please try again. For password reset please contact the service administrator.
This app has been disabled for your service. Please contact your service administrator.

stateMatrix.trigger: Account lacks access to the app
stateMatrix.copy: Your account doesn't have access to this app. Contact your service administrator to request access.
Something went wrong loading the educator list. Check your connection and try again.
Too many incorrect attempts. Contact your service administrator to reset your PIN.

stateMatrix.trigger: No rooms set up for the service
stateMatrix.copy: There are no rooms set up for this service yet. Contact your service administrator.
<Room> is closed today. You can still enter if you need to.

stateMatrix.trigger: Educator uses password auth instead of PIN
stateMatrix.ui: email + password fields, 'Use PIN instead' link

stateMatrix.copy: Something went wrong while loading. Please try again.

platformDeltas.android: Jetpack Navigation Compose; equivalent back stack
resources / Compose theme from the same token values

platformDeltas.ios: iOS 17+ assumed — iOS 16 support is an OPEN question (~2,000 field devices below 17)

openQuestions.q: 'Forgot password?' link colour is off-convention in the Centred-classic direction (purple accent-secondary vs the teal text-link convention) — normalise to teal, or add an accent-link token?
Confirm iOS minimum deployment target — iOS 17+ vs iOS 16 support for ~2,000 field devices (affects SwiftUI APIs).
Does the service step need an alternate 'passcode instead' affordance? (Only direction 4 surfaced it; Centred classic does not.)
PIN lockout threshold — how many incorrect attempts before e-locked, and what is the reset path?

decisions.rationale: Most conventional, lowest-risk treatment; white centred column on Stardust tokens. Other directions remain in the comparison round.

changelog.change: Initial handoff — Centred classic direction, phone + iPad, iOS + Android.
